title: alnus viridis subsp. sinuata
intro: Alnus viridis subsp. sinuata, also known as mountain alder, is a subspecies of tree belonging to the alder family (Alnaceae) and is native to North America. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Alnus viridis subsp. sinuata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various ailments such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain, and promote wound healing.
